text: Code refactoring is the process of improving your code's internal structure without changing its external behavior. Think of it like tidying up a messy room: you reorganize things, but the room's purpose stays the same. By refactoring, you make the code cleaner, easier to read, and simpler to maintain. This helps reduce technical debt—the cost of fixing quick, poorly designed solutions later. Regular refactoring ensures your codebase stays efficient and adaptable.
